figures.caption: Table 1. Emergence from agentic affordance and emergence from multi-agent interaction.

Figure 1. The generative safety pipeline. Stage 1 identifies macro-level risk phenomena (e.g., collusion, polarization). Stage 2 formulates testable hypotheses about local interaction rules sufficient to generate them. Stage 3 implements these configurations in controlled multi-agent simulations. Stage 4 uses the same environment to test interventions targeting model behavior or interaction architecture. Stage 5 validates results against empirical baselines. The dashed arrow indicates iteration between experimentation and intervention design.
